description: Fix YouTube video player error 153 on results page and improve SecureYouTubePlayer fallback behavior
steps: In src/components/ui/SecureYouTubePlayer.tsx, the player tries to use the YouTube IFrame API with controls=0 and sandbox restrictions. Error 153 usually means the video cannot be played in an embedded player due to restrictions. The fix should: (1) Remove the sandbox attribute from the API-mode iframe since it blocks the YouTube API from working properly. (2) Make the fallback mode (which shows native YouTube controls) the default approach instead of trying the complex custom controls API first. (3) Add proper error handling - if the video URL is just a regular YouTube link, show it embedded with native controls. The key insight is that sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' is too restrictive for YouTube API.

Simplify the component to use a reliable approach: always use youtube-nocookie.com/embed with native controls (modestbranding=1&rel=0&controls=1). Remove the complex YouTube IFrame API code that causes the error 153. Keep the title bar and close button overlay.
Test that the general video solution button on the results page now opens a working YouTube embed without error 153
acceptance_criteria: SecureYouTubePlayer renders a working YouTube embed without error 153
The player uses youtube-nocookie.com for privacy
The player shows the title bar and close button
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run npm run build and confirm no TypeScript errors
findings: Component simplified successfully. Removed YouTube IFrame API code, sandbox attribute, and custom control overlay. Now uses native YouTube controls via youtube-nocookie.com/embed. Build passes cleanly. npm install was required to set up node_modules and prisma client.
